research.interface: Jira Cloud platform REST API v3 project archive/restore, asynchronous project delete, and the long-running task resource

official_sources.title: The Jira Cloud platform REST API — official OpenAPI v3 specification
official_sources.used_for: Archive project: POST /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey}/archive, 204 on success, and the prerequisite rule 'You can't delete a project if it's archived. To delete an archived project, restore the project and then delete it.'; Restore project: POST /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey}/restore, 200 — 'Restores a project that has been archived or placed in the Jira recycle bin'; Delete project asynchronously: POST /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey}/delete — 'This operation is: transactional, that is, if part of the delete fails the project is not deleted' and 'asynchronous. Follow the location link in the response... use Get task to obtain subsequent updates', 303 response; Get task: GET /rest/api/3/task/{taskId} with TaskProgressBeanObject {self, id, status ENQUEUED/RUNNING/COMPLETE/FAILED/CANCEL_REQUESTED/CANCELLED/DEAD, progress, message, result, lastUpdate, submitted, submittedBy, elapsedRuntime}; Project schema archived/deleted read-only booleans; ErrorCollection {errorMessages, errors}

official_sources.title: The Jira Cloud platform REST API — About (v3 intro)
official_sources.used_for: the Asynchronous operations contract: 'the operation will schedule an asynchronous task and return a 303 (See Other) response, indicating the location of the queued task in the Location header. You can query this task to get progress updates. When the task finishes, the response object will contain the result field'; and 'asynchronous tasks are not guaranteed to be run in order'

official_sources.title: Basic auth for REST APIs — Atlassian Developer
official_sources.used_for: Authorization: Basic base64(email:api_token) — the Atlassian account email joined with an API token by a colon, base64-encoded; API tokens instead of passwords

research.language_rationale: The retention pipeline this feature extends is a zero-dependency Java tool built on java.net.http, and the catalog item pins raw wire behavior — the 303 See Other with its Location header (java.net.http must be configured to NOT auto-follow), the TaskProgressBeanObject status machine, and the archived-project prerequisite — that Atlassian's Java REST client generations abstract away. The JDK's HttpClient plus the tool's existing minimal JSON reader keeps every documented detail observable.

verified_facts: Archive project is POST /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey}/archive answering 204 with no body; archived and deleted are read-only booleans on the v3 project resource (absent on an active project).

The official prerequisite runs opposite to folk wisdom: 'You can't delete a project if it's archived. To delete an archived project, restore the project and then delete it.' — so a purge of an archived project must POST /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey}/restore (200) before any delete.

Delete project asynchronously is POST /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey}/delete; it is transactional ('if part of the delete fails the project is not deleted') and asynchronous: it answers 303 (See Other) with the queued task's URL in the Location header.

Task progress is read from GET /rest/api/3/task/{taskId} returning TaskProgressBeanObject: status is one of ENQUEUED, RUNNING, COMPLETE, FAILED, CANCEL_REQUESTED, CANCELLED, DEAD; progress is a percentage; result carries the operation-specific outcome once finished.
Asynchronous tasks 'are not guaranteed to be run in order' — poll observations (progress, lastUpdate) may regress between reads, so only the status field is authoritative for termination.

The synchronous DELETE /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey} (with its enableUndo recycle-bin semantics) is a different operation from the asynchronous POST .../delete and is not part of this workflow.
Failures return ErrorCollection {errorMessages, errors}; authentication is HTTP basic: base64 of email:api_token in the Authorization header.
